zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Stones於1598年10月27日出生在英國英格蘭爾阿爾德利時，她的父親William Stones20歲，母親Judith Brown19歲。她於1616年4月9日與Lt Robert Vinton在英國英格蘭賀特福爾聖 邁克爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她於1598年住在英國爾維頓且於1603年住在英國英格蘭爾。她去世於1660年在美國麻薩諸塞州普萊姆斯希圖阿潔享年62歲。

nameMeanings.name: Stones
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of Stone with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. A number of the places called Stone are sometimes recorded with plural spellings in the medieval period, including Stone (Staffordshire), recorded as Stanes in 1187.

variants.name: Stone
meanings: English: from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone, rock’ (Old English stān). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on stony ground, by a notable outcrop of rock, or by a stone boundary-marker or monument, or habitational, from a place called Stone, such as those in Buckinghamshire, Devon, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Staffordshire, and Worcestershire.
Irish (Kilkenny): adopted for Irish Ó Clochartaigh (see Clougherty ) and/or Ó Clochasaigh (see Clohessy ), and possibly several other names containing or thought to contain the element cloch ‘stone’.
Americanized form (translation into English) of various surnames in other languages, meaning ‘stone’, including Jewish Stein , Norwegian Steine, French Lapierre .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Stones was born on 27 October 1598, in Alderley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Stones, was 20 and her mother, Judith Brown, was 19. She married Lt Robert Vinton on 9 April 1616, in Saint Michael, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She lived in Witton, Cheshire, England in 1598 and Cheshire, England, United Kingdom in 1603. She died in 1660, in Scituate, Plymouth, Massachusetts, United States, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Stones est née le 27 octobre 1598, à Alderley, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Stones, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Judith Brown, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Lt Robert Vinton le 9 avril 1616, à Saint Michael, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle a vécu à Witton, Cheshire, Angleterre en 1598 et à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni en 1603. Elle est décédée en 1660, à Scituate, Plymouth, Massachusetts, États-Unis, à l’âge de 62 ans.

April 1616 in Saint Michael, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom Lt Robert Vinton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. 1598 hat sie in Witton, Cheshire, England und 1603 in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom gelebt. Sie starb 1660 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Scituate, Plymouth, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Stones è nata il il 27 ottobre 1598 nel Alderley, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William Stones, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Judith Brown, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato Lt Robert Vinton il 9 aprile 1616, nel Saint Michael, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Vissuto a Witton, Cheshire, England nel 1598 e nel Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito nel 1603. Morì nel 1660, nel Scituate, Plymouth, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Stonesが1598年10月27日にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアアルダーリーで生まれた時，父William Stonesは20歳，母Judith Brownは19歳であった。1616年4月9日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサイント ミチャエルでLt Robert Vintonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1598年にはイギリスチェシュアイアウィットオンに，1603年にはイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアに住んでいた。彼女は1660年にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州プリモウススシトゥアトで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Stones(이)가 1598년 10월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 알델리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Stones(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Judith Brown(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1616년 4월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 세인트 믹하얼에서 Lt Robert Vinton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1598년도에 영국 체실르 윗튼에서, 1603년도에 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 살았다. 그녀는 1660년 미국 매사추세츠 주 플림우스 스시투앳에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Lt Robert Vinton em 9 de abril de 1616, em Saint Michael, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Stones родилась 27 октября 1598 г. в местности алдирли, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William Stones было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Judith Brown, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lt Robert Vinton 9 апреля 1616 г. в местности сеинт мачаел, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она жила в местности виттон, чешир, Англия в 1598 году и в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания в 1603 году. Она умерла в 1660 г. в местности сситуэит, плимоут, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Stones nació el 27 de octubre de 1598, en Alderley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William Stones, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Judith Brown, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Lt Robert Vinton el 9 de abril de 1616, en Saint Michael, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Vivió en Witton, Cheshire, Inglaterra en 1598 y en Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido en 1603. Murió en 1660, en Scituate, Plymouth, Masachusets, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 62 años.
